name: react-router
summary: Declarative client-side routing for React — maps URLs to components with nested routes, loaders, and actions.

story.problem: A React app is a single HTML page. Clicking a link reloads the page and loses all state. You need URL changes to render different components without full reloads, while keeping the address bar in sync, supporting back/forward, and handling nested layouts.
story.mental_model: React Router maps URL patterns to component trees. A <Route path='/users/:id'> renders when the URL matches. Nested routes render inside their parent's <Outlet>. v6.4+ adds loaders (fetch data before render) and actions (handle form submissions) — making it a full request/response model.
story.when_to_use: Any React SPA needing URL-based navigation, nested layouts (dashboard sidebar + content), data loading tied to routes, form actions, protected routes.
story.when_not_to_use: Next.js (use its file-based router). Remix (built on React Router but adds SSR). Very simple apps with only one or two views — a state variable suffices.

alternatives.name: tanstack-router
alternatives.reason: Fully type-safe routes, file-based or code-based, excellent TypeScript DX.

alternatives.name: wouter
alternatives.reason: Tiny (1.5kB) React Router alternative, great for small projects.

alternatives.name: next.js router
alternatives.reason: File-based, SSR-first. Use if you're already on Next.js.

nodes.label: react-router
nodes.summary: Import createBrowserRouter, RouterProvider, Route, Link, etc.

nodes.label: createBrowserRouter(routes)
nodes.summary: Modern router factory. Define route tree as array of objects. Supports loaders/actions.

nodes.label: RouterProvider
nodes.summary: <RouterProvider router={router} /> — mounts the router. Replaces old <BrowserRouter>.

nodes.label: Route objects
nodes.summary: { path, element, loader, action, children, errorElement, index }.

nodes.label: <Outlet />
nodes.summary: Renders matched child route inside a layout component. The key to nested layouts.

nodes.label: <Link> / <NavLink>
nodes.summary: <Link to='/about'> for navigation. <NavLink> adds active class when route matches.

nodes.label: useNavigate / useParams / useLoaderData
nodes.summary: useNavigate() for imperative nav, useParams() for :id params, useLoaderData() for loader results.

nodes.label: loader (data fetching)
nodes.summary: async function loader({ params }) — runs before component renders. Return data, redirect, or Response.

tasks.title: Set up routing in a React app

steps.label: Define routes
steps.code:
import { createBrowserRouter, RouterProvider } from 'react-router'

const router = createBrowserRouter([
  { path: '/', element: <Home /> },
  { path: '/about', element: <About /> },
  { path: '/users/:id', element: <UserProfile /> },
])

steps.label: Layout component
Nested routes
Define loader
Consume in component
